page: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor with 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core via hyperthreading. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is provided by a local 100 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is in the top tier (top 10%), and single-threaded compression and decompression tasks register strong results in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance falls into the average category, and Passmark CPU tests (including physics, encryption, and extended instructions) score in the weak bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average, but small-block cached read bandwidth is weak. Database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput are also weak to poor, with web serving reaching only 2.3 GB/sec.

Given the shared CPU allocation and 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated multi-core compute power. The high memory capacity relative to the low vCPU count makes it suitable for caching layers, lightweight memory-intensive databases, or background workers. However, it is poorly suited for high-throughput web serving, intensive database operations, or complex multi-threaded processing due to its shared CPU architecture and weak multi-core benchmark results.
description: The Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor. It features 2 shared vCPUs (1 physical core with hyperthreading) running at 2.0 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While single-core stress-ng and single-threaded compression benchmarks place this server in the top tier, its multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are average. Furthermore, database operations, Redis SET throughput, and static web serving are weak, with web serving limited to 2.3 GB/sec. With a high 8.0 GB of memory per core, this instance represents a cost-efficient option for memory-optimized workloads that do not demand sustained CPU performance. It is best suited for caching layers, lightweight memory-intensive applications, and basic background processing.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd is a memory-optimized server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 100 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance but weak database and web serving throughput.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd features 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 100 GB NVMe SSD, suitable for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized shared instance combining 16 GB of RAM with local NVMe storage for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with 2 shared vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
100 GB local NVMe SSD storage included
Top-tier single-core CPU and strong single-threaded compression performance
Weak database, Redis, and static web serving throughput
Suitable for caching layers and lightweight memory-intensive workloads
